reasoning: The time series data for 30-year expected inflation from the Cleveland Fed (EXPINF30YR) shows recent upward momentum over the last few months, growing from 2.41% in August to 2.45% in January. Although short-term momentum is upward, long-term trends have been flat or slightly down since the start of 2025 (dropping from 2.53% to 2.45%). The statistical model suggests a combined probability of 0.47 based on the linear trend and seasonal components. Given the near-random walk nature of financial market-implied inflation expectations on a one-month horizon, the probability is very close to 50%. I adjust slightly down to 49% reflecting the mean-reverting nature of expected inflation over longer horizons and the statistical combined estimate.
